patch.value:
## Context
The product philosophy is "project management = context management": the tracker must let an agent rebuild optimal working context at any moment, at any workspace scale. The building blocks exist as separate backlog items — pm-4k6b relevance engine, pm-5qmm memory tiers, pm-atfm quality harness, pm-hib1 point-in-time reads, pm focus, learnings/decisions, append-only history streams — but no recorded decision states the unifying model they compose into. The stated long-term goal (agents building near-human context continuity from pm alone) needs an explicit architecture so future context features strengthen one coherent system instead of accreting ad-hoc signals.

## Proposal
Model agent context assembly on the architecture of human memory. Each subsystem maps to a pm primitive that already exists or is on the roadmap:

- **Working memory (attention):** pm focus + token-budgeted context/next assembly (pm-4k6b) — the small, high-relevance active set an agent holds "in mind".
- **Episodic memory:** per-item append-only history streams + point-in-time reads (pm-hib1) — what happened, when, by whom; immutable and replayable.
- **Semantic memory:** learnings, decisions/ADRs, and the knowledge-density ranking signal (pm-4k6b) — distilled facts that outlive the episodes that produced them.
- **Procedural memory:** plans, templates, profiles, schema — how this workspace does things.
- **Consolidation:** derived closed-epoch rollups (pm-5qmm) + history-compact checkpoints — episodic detail summarizes into semantic form as it ages, in bounded tokens.
- **Associative recall:** dependency-graph proximity + hybrid semantic retrieval (pm-4k6b) — related context surfaces by association with the focus item, not by enumeration.
- **Graceful forgetting:** relevance decay and projection degradation under a token budget — never deletion. Under the immutable-history invariant, "forgotten" only means "not loaded"; total recall stays possible via restore, point-in-time reads, and attestation (pm-3z0k).

## Decision drivers
- One mental model: every new context feature must name which memory subsystem it extends, keeping the engine coherent as it grows.
- Spectrum invariance: the same architecture serves a 3-item minutes-old scratch workspace (pm-z436) and a million-item decades-spanning one (pm-g2zz); only budgets and consolidation depth change.
- SDK-first (ADR pm-muhw): every subsystem is a public SDK primitive; CLI and MCP are presentation layers.
- Explainability: recall must be auditable (per-signal score breakdown in pm-4k6b), never oracle-like.

## Consequences (if accepted)
- pm-4k6b, pm-5qmm, and pm-atfm acceptance criteria reference their subsystem role; pm-atfm gains continuity ("returning agent") scenario classes.
- Docs gain a context-architecture page tying the philosophy to the concrete primitives (task to be filed on acceptance).
- The returning-agent continuity story becomes the end-to-end acceptance narrative for the composed system.

Status: proposed.
ADR: cognitive memory architecture — agent context assembly modeled on human memory systems (proposed)

Unifying design decision for the context engine: working/episodic/semantic/procedural memory, consolidation, associative recall, and graceful forgetting each map to a named pm primitive. Dedupe-checked: no existing cognitive/memory-architecture decision item.

ADR reviewed and moved to accepted or rejected with rationale recorded; if accepted: the mapping table names one owning implementation item per memory subsystem, pm-atfm scenario classes include returning-agent continuity cases, and a docs context-architecture page task is filed

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Subsystem-map addition proposed: pm-uwfs (context usage feedback signal) supplies the one mechanism this ADR's seven-subsystem map omits — memory strengthening through recall (retrieval-practice effect). If this ADR is accepted, add it as the eighth mapping: served-then-acted-on outcomes -> usage-affinity scoring input in pm-4k6b, with the existing graceful-forgetting decay as the complementary weakening term. Exploration/anti-lock-in guard is stated in pm-uwfs acceptance criteria.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Planning pass 2026-07-07: two candidate subsystems to complete the human-memory mapping (file as items only on ADR acceptance):
(1) Prospective memory — remembering to act in the future. Primitives exist (Reminder/Event types, start_at/end_at, deadline signal in pm-3hps) but are not yet composed: due/imminent scheduled items should surface in context/next automatically when temporally relevant, without the agent polling a calendar. Depends on pm-x1g5 (get omits schedule facet) being fixed first.
(2) Metacognition — knowing what you do not know. The confidence metadata field should modulate recall, not just describe items: low-confidence decisions/learnings get resurfaced for revalidation when their subject matter re-enters the working set (semantic-match trigger), and a bounded "known unknowns" projection lists load-bearing low-confidence context. Complements pm-uwfs (retrieval practice strengthens; metacognition flags what needs re-checking).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Planning pass 2026-07-07 (follow-up): third candidate subsystem to complete the human-memory mapping (same convention — file as an item only on ADR acceptance):
(3) Interference & belief revision — human memory resolves contradictions between new and old knowledge instead of serving both. pm mapping, two halves: (a) write-time conflict advisory — when a new learning/decision is semantically near-identical to an existing one but points the other way, surface the older entry before the write lands (extends the pm-4ri6 create-time near-duplicate advisory from items to knowledge entries; same hybrid-similarity machinery, different corpus); (b) supersedence as a first-class relation — a decision/learning can record supersedes/superseded_by so context assembly serves the current belief and demotes (never deletes) the superseded one; the full trail stays replayable via immutable history. Complements #10 metacognition: conflict detection flags WHAT to revalidate, confidence modulation decides WHEN. ADR-practice precedent: superseded status is standard for ADR lifecycles and pm-m2u1 (living ADR log) currently has no supersedence edge.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Planning pass 2026-07-07 (second follow-up): fourth candidate subsystem for the human-memory mapping (same convention — file as an item only on ADR acceptance):
(4) Novelty detection / orienting response — human attention is drawn preferentially to what CHANGED since it last looked; a returning agent's highest-value context is the delta it has not yet seen. pm mapping: derive a per-author watermark from the agent's own last authored history entry (zero new state — pure derivation from the immutable episodic streams), then treat changed-by-OTHERS-since-watermark as a salience signal in context/next assembly, with the delta summarized in bounded tokens ("catching up" projection). Distinct from pm-4k6b author affinity (affinity ranks what I touched; novelty surfaces what I have NOT seen change) and strictly beyond closed pm-ankr/pm-y138 (those shipped manual --updated-since windows; here the system derives the watermark itself, so the agent never has to remember its own last visit). Composes episodic memory (history streams) into working-memory assembly; complements #1 prospective memory (time-triggered) with change-triggered attention. Small-workspace neutral: with one author and no interleaved writers the delta is empty and the signal is silent.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Planning pass 2026-07-07 (third follow-up): fifth candidate subsystem for the human-memory mapping (same convention — file as an item only on ADR acceptance):
(5) Theory of mind / common ground — humans model what OTHER minds already know and communicate only the delta; effective multi-agent coordination needs the same. pm mapping: every agent's knowledge horizon is already derivable from the immutable episodic streams (the watermark of candidate #4, but computed for ANY author, not just self). Two compositions: (a) handoff briefs — when work moves between agents (claim transfer, review request, plan step reassignment), assemble context relative to the RECEIVER's watermark, so a claim handoff costs only the tokens of what the receiver has not seen; (b) expertise routing — 'who knows about X' answered by episodic-trace density per author over the semantic neighborhood of X (which agent authored/touched the most relevant history), turning authorship provenance into a coordination primitive for workspaces with thousands of concurrent agents. Zero new stored state — pure derivation from history streams plus the hybrid index. Distinct from #4 novelty detection (self-directed catching-up) and from pm-4k6b author affinity (ranking MY items); this is other-directed: minimizing what must be transmitted between minds. Small-workspace neutral: single-author workspaces derive an empty delta and the subsystem is silent.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Planning pass 2026-07-07 (fourth follow-up): two further candidate subsystems for the human-memory mapping (same convention — file as items only on ADR acceptance):
(6) Source monitoring — humans track not just what they know but HOW they came to know it (perceived vs told vs inferred), and weight trust accordingly. pm mapping: grade every semantic-memory entry (learning, decision, resolution) by a derived evidence class — test-verified (linked pm test run passed) > resolution-verified (structured expected+actual recorded) > externally-corroborated (linked GH issue/PR closed) > agent-asserted (comment/note only) > inferred. The class is PURE derivation from history streams, linked test runs, and attestation (pm-3z0k) — zero new stored state — and it complements, not duplicates, #2 metacognition: confidence is what the author SELF-reported; evidence class is what the record can PROVE. Recall composition: pm-4k6b knowledge-density signal weights entries by evidence class, and --explain-ranking shows the class so an agent knows whether it is acting on proof or hearsay. Spectrum-neutral: single-class workspaces rank exactly as today.
(7) Negativity bias / one-shot avoidance — humans weight failure memories asymmetrically and re-trigger them at DECISION time, not recall time. pm mapping: when a mutation or plan step enters a semantic neighborhood dense in failure-outcome episodes (closed issues whose resolution records a regression/incident, learnings tagged from post-mortems), resurface the strongest such episode as a bounded cautionary advisory BEFORE the write lands — the write-path analog of the pm-4ri6 create-time near-duplicate advisory, reusing the same hybrid-similarity machinery over a failure-filtered corpus. Distinct from pm-4k6b knowledge density (symmetric, assembly-time ranking): this is asymmetric (failures outweigh successes) and mutation-triggered. Complements #3 belief revision (contradiction-triggered) and #6 (advisory strength scales with evidence class). Spectrum-neutral: no failure episodes near the action means no advisory.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Planning pass 2026-07-07 (fifth follow-up): eighth candidate subsystem for the human-memory mapping (same convention — file as an item only on ADR acceptance):
(8) Habit formation / procedural acquisition — the body's procedural memory (plans, templates, profiles, schema) is entirely HAND-AUTHORED; humans additionally acquire procedures implicitly, by repetition. pm mapping: mine the episodic streams for recurring cross-item action sequences by the same author cohort (e.g. create->claim->test link->close-with-resolution->changelog regen, or a recurring triage sequence) and, past a repetition threshold, surface a bounded suggestion to distill the pattern into an authored artifact (template, plan blueprint, or profile) that agents then get served from procedural memory instead of re-deriving the sequence each session. Pure derivation from history streams (n-gram / episode-mining over per-author mutation sequences; the pm-4k6b write-time fold (6) is the natural place to maintain sequence counts) — zero new stored state until a human/agent ACCEPTS the suggestion, at which point the artifact is normal authored data. Distinct from consolidation (episodic->semantic distills FACTS; this distills ACTION SEQUENCES) and from pm-uwfs retrieval practice (which strengthens recall of existing entries; this creates new procedural artifacts). NO daemon per ADR pm-i7i4: counts fold at write time, suggestions render on-demand in context/validate output. Spectrum-neutral: below the repetition threshold (small/young workspaces) the subsystem is silent.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Planning pass 2026-07-07 (sixth follow-up): ninth candidate subsystem for the human-memory mapping (same convention — file as an item only on ADR acceptance):
(9) Encoding specificity / context-dependent recall — human recall improves sharply when the retrieval context matches the encoding context (Tulving: what you can remember depends on the cues present when you stored it). pm mapping: every mutation already lands in the item's history stream, but the WRITE-TIME SITUATION is mostly discarded — which focus item was active, which plan step was executing, which files were linked/touched, which branch/session the author was in. Two halves: (a) encoding — stamp a compact situation snapshot (focus id, active plan step, linked-file set hash, session/branch tag) into history entries at mutation time, reusing the pm focus session-context primitive that already exists; zero new commands, one optional envelope field. (b) retrieval — a match-between-then-and-now signal in the pm-4k6b scorer: items whose encoding situation overlaps the CURRENT retrieval situation (same files in play, same plan lineage, same focus subtree) get boosted, so an agent returning to a file or plan step preferentially recalls exactly what was learned the last time that situation held. Distinct from semantic similarity (matches CONTENT, not situation), from author affinity (matches WHO, not where/when-in-what-state), and from novelty #4 (what changed vs what situationally co-occurred). Spectrum-neutral: small workspaces have few distinct situations so the signal degenerates gracefully to recency; at millions of items it is a sharp filter that no content-based signal can replicate. Derived-never-authoritative holds: situation stamps are optional envelope data in the immutable stream; the match index folds at write time (pm-4k6b algorithm (6)) and rebuilds from history.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Planning pass 2026-07-07 (seventh follow-up): tenth candidate subsystem for the human-memory mapping (same convention — file as an item only on ADR acceptance):
(10) Chunking / expert compression — working memory holds only ~4-7 units regardless of unit SIZE, so experts compress many low-level elements into one higher-order chunk and recall the chunk, expanding detail only on demand. pm mapping: the hierarchy (milestone -> epic -> story/feature -> task) already defines chunk boundaries, and pm-gcm3 shipped the primitive (child rollup summary on get for Milestone/Epic); what is missing is retrieval-side composition — when the token budget cannot afford N sibling items, context assembly serves ONE derived parent chunk (bounded rollup: counts by status, top-k salient children per the scorer, one-line digest) with drill-down pointers, instead of truncating the sibling list arbitrarily. Distinct from consolidation (pm-5qmm epoch rollups compress by AGE over closed items; chunking compresses by STRUCTURE over live context at any age) and from token-budget packing pm-4k6b (4) (packing selects WHICH flat items fit; chunking changes the UNIT being packed). Derived-never-authoritative holds: rollups fold at write time (pm-4k6b (6)) and rebuild from the immutable history streams. Spectrum behavior: small workspaces never hit the budget, so everything serves at full granularity; million-item workspaces read as a small set of chunks that expand on focus — exactly how an expert perceives a large system. Scorer-side counterpart: pm-4k6b algorithm (9) granularity-adaptive packing.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Candidate subsystem (11) analogical retrieval / structure mapping. Human experts recall precedents by RELATIONAL STRUCTURE, not surface content (Gentner's structure-mapping): "a blocker closed as wontfix while a dependent release deadline approached" retrieves the past episode with the same dependency-and-outcome shape even when the items share no vocabulary. Today the tracker has only two recall channels for past work: semantic similarity (embedding/BM25 over text) and associative connectivity (spreading activation over explicit edges, pm-4k6b algorithm 2). Both miss structural precedent: an agent facing a novel-looking situation whose SHAPE (typed subgraph of deps/parent/status-transitions/close-reasons around the focus item) matches a resolved past episode gets no signal. Proposed subsystem: at recall time, extract the focus item's local relational neighborhood as a typed graph, retrieve top-k past episodes with the highest structural-match score, and surface them as "precedents" with their resolutions/learnings — giving agents case-based reasoning over the full history. Distinct from (4) novelty detection (which fires on NO match; this fires on deep-structure match under surface mismatch), from (9) encoding specificity (situation stamps are write-time literals; this is shape abstraction), and from (10) chunking (compression, not retrieval). Needs pm-4k6b algorithm (10) structural fingerprints as its index; consumes close_reason/resolution fields (pm-96wm lineage) as the precedent payload. Acceptance shape when promoted to an item: precedent recall surfaced in pm context for items whose structural match score clears a threshold, with the matched shape explained (which relations aligned), never raw dumps.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Planning pass 2026-07-07 (eighth follow-up): twelfth candidate subsystem for the human-memory mapping (same convention — file as an item only on ADR acceptance):
(12) Open-loop persistence / Zeigarnik effect — incomplete tasks hold elevated, intrusive activation in human memory until completed or deliberately deferred; completion releases the tension sharply (Zeigarnik/Ovsiankina), and even just forming a concrete deferral plan releases it without completion (Masicampo & Baumeister). pm mapping: an agent's open loops — claimed WIP, unresolved Decisions, questions raised in notes without answers, reviews awaiting reply — must not decay out of assembled context under recency scoring the way ordinary items do; they carry a persistence floor until a closure event (close, claim release, explicit parking via blocked-with-reason) releases it. Today the tracker treats WIP like any other item at retrieval time: pm-w8q4 stale-WIP detection alerts on hygiene AFTER work has gone stale, and pm focus holds an explicit session set, but nothing gives incomplete work automatic retrieval persistence between sessions — an agent resuming after interruption should find its own open loops leading context, unprompted. Distinct from (1) prospective memory (future-conditioned intentions, when-X-do-Y; this is present-tense unfinished work), from (8) habit formation (procedural shortcuts), and from stale-WIP hygiene (detection vs retrieval-side persistence). Explicit parking is first-class: deferral with a recorded reason releases tension WITHOUT closure, matching the human finding that a concrete plan quiets intrusion. Scorer-side counterpart: pm-4k6b algorithm (11) open-loop tension with decay floors. Spectrum behavior: tiny workspaces fit everything in context so the floor is invisible; thousand-agent workspaces get per-agent working sets that always lead with that agent's own unfinished business at zero scan cost. Acceptance shape when promoted to an item: context assembly serves an agent's open loops above the fold with the tension explained (claimed 3d ago, blocks 2, decision pending), and closure/parking events demonstrably release the floor.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Planning pass 2026-07-08: thirteenth candidate subsystem for the human-memory mapping (same convention — file as an item only on ADR acceptance): (13) Episodic future thinking / constructive simulation — humans pre-experience future episodes by RECOMBINING fragments of past episodes (Schacter & Addis: remembering and imagining share one constructive machinery), which is how experts run pre-mortems: before acting they simulate how this specific plan tends to fail. pm mapping: before an agent starts a claimed item or materializes a plan, assemble a forward-looking context block from three derived sources that all exist already — (a) precedent episodes: top-k structurally similar past work items via analogical retrieval (candidate 11 / algorithm 10 WL fingerprints), conditioned on OUTCOME (close_reason, resolution, reopen count, expected-vs-actual divergence) so the simulation surfaces how similar work actually ended, not just that it existed; (b) blast radius: the dependency/parent/file-link neighborhood weighted by historical co-change statistics mined from episodic streams (algorithm 12), predicting which items/files this work will likely touch beyond its declared edges; (c) unresolved hazards: open loops (candidate 12) and negativity-weighted precedents (candidate 7) inside that predicted radius. Surface as a compact 'if you start this, expect...' section in pm context/pm next envelopes and as suggested risks/validation steps in pm plan. Distinct from prospective memory (candidate 1: time-triggered reminders) — this is content-triggered SIMULATION of the episode ahead; and distinct from plain analogical recall (candidate 11) by being outcome-conditioned and projected onto the current dependency neighborhood. Composes entirely from immutable history + deps + WL fingerprints; no new storage required beyond the co-change fold (algorithm 12 sketch state).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Planning pass 2026-07-08 (follow-up): fourteenth candidate subsystem for the human-memory mapping (same convention — file as an item only on ADR acceptance): (14) Global workspace / conscious broadcast — in Global Workspace Theory (Baars; Dehaene's global neuronal workspace), specialized processors work in parallel and unconsciously, but a limited-capacity workspace lets the single most salient event win a competition and be BROADCAST to all processors at once, which is how a distributed mind acts on one thing coherently. pm mapping for thousands of concurrent agents: most mutations should stay local (an agent's own lane), but some events objectively matter to everyone touching the neighborhood — a blocker closed as wontfix under a release deadline, a security learning, a dependency cycle introduced, a hot file about to be co-edited by three lanes. Proposed subsystem: events emitted into the immutable streams (substrate: pm-e200 event stream, no daemon per ADR pm-i7i4) compete for a bounded workspace-wide broadcast channel; winners are injected as a compact 'workspace attention' block into EVERY agent's next pm context / pm next envelope until acknowledged or decayed. Losers stay retrievable but unbroadcast. Distinct from (5) theory of mind (receiver-initiated PULL: assemble delta relative to one receiver's watermark; this is arbitration-initiated PUSH: one salient event reaches all receivers), from (4) novelty detection (self-directed catch-up on one's own horizon), and from (1) prospective memory (time/condition-triggered per-item reminders, not competition for shared attention). Composes with pm-4k6b algorithm (13) for the arbitration math; consumes open-loop tension (12), co-change blast radius (algorithm 12), and negativity weighting (7) as salience inputs. Small-workspace behavior: with one agent the broadcast channel degenerates to a self-note and stays silent below threshold — zero cost, zero noise. Acceptance shape when promoted: bounded broadcast block (working-memory capacity, ~4 slots) in context envelopes, each entry one line with its salience explanation, acknowledgment recorded as an ordinary immutable event.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Planning pass 2026-07-08 (second follow-up): fifteenth candidate subsystem for the human-memory mapping (same convention — file as an item only on ADR acceptance): (15) Memory reconsolidation / updating — in humans, retrieving a consolidated memory transiently returns it to a labile state where it can be UPDATED and must be re-stabilised; the act of recall is also an act of rewrite, so remembering under new circumstances edits what will be remembered next time (Nader; Lee). This is the missing complement to (14) global-workspace broadcast (which decides WHAT reaches everyone) and (11) analogical retrieval (which decides WHAT precedent surfaces): reconsolidation decides HOW a surfaced precedent is CORRECTED by the present. pm mapping: when an agent retrieves an old item/precedent as context and its current work contradicts or refines that precedent (a decision that turned out wrong, an estimate that was 3x off, a 'fix' that regressed), the retrieval event should open a bounded window in which the agent can attach a reconsolidation delta — an immutable, append-only correction note tied to BOTH the original item and the retrieving context — so the NEXT retrieval of that precedent surfaces the correction alongside it, never silently overwriting the original (which stays intact per the immutability invariant). Distinct from ordinary comments/notes (unstructured, not retrieval-gated, not paired to a triggering context) and from (4) novelty detection (self-catch-up, no source edit). The subsystem makes the corpus SELF-CORRECTING under use: precedents that keep getting contradicted accrue visible correction pressure and decay in retrieval rank, while confirmed ones harden — mirroring how human memory strengthens what is re-consolidated consistently and revises what is re-consolidated in conflict. Composes with pm-4k6b: needs a retrieval-conditioned update-eligibility signal (algorithm below) so reconsolidation only fires on genuine contradiction, not on every read (over-firing would make the corpus thrash). Small-project behavior: with few items and few retrievals, the window rarely opens and costs nothing; large-project behavior: correction deltas become a first-class, queryable signal of which decisions/estimates the organisation keeps relitigating. Acceptance shape when promoted: retrieval emits an eligibility flag; an optional reconsolidation delta is an ordinary immutable event pairing (original_id, retrieving_context_id, correction); default retrieval surfaces the original plus its N most recent corrections; original bytes never mutate.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Planning pass 2026-07-09 (third follow-up): sixteenth candidate subsystem for the human-memory mapping (same convention — file as an item only on ADR acceptance): (16) Fuzzy-trace dual representation — gist vs verbatim with DIFFERENTIAL decay (Reyna & Brainerd). In humans a memory is encoded as two parallel traces: a VERBATIM trace (surface detail, exact wording, transient specifics) and a GIST trace (semantic meaning, the durable "what it was about"). The verbatim trace decays FAST; the gist trace persists for years; and retrieval preferentially reconstructs from gist, which is why people reliably recall the meaning of an event long after the specifics are gone. This is the STORAGE-lifecycle complement to the three read-time subsystems already mapped: (10) chunking groups siblings, algorithm (9) granularity-adaptive packing SELECTS a representation level at read time under a budget, and (15) reconsolidation CORRECTS a surfaced precedent — but none of them govern how an item's OWN detail should age. pm mapping: every item already carries a natural verbatim/gist split — verbatim = full body + comments + raw history events + exact estimates; gist = title + resolution + close_reason + outcome (expected/actual) + a short durable digest. The subsystem says these two layers should have DIFFERENT retention policies: verbatim detail on old terminal items is a legitimate candidate for gist-preserving compaction (history-compact territory) once the item is closed and quiescent, while the gist layer is retained permanently and NEVER compacted — so a decade-old closed item still surfaces its meaning cheaply even after its verbatim body is checkpointed away. Crucially this must preserve the immutability invariant: verbatim is never destroyed, only MOVED behind a compaction checkpoint that remains replayable (history-compact already guarantees replay integrity), so "get --at <version>" can always reconstruct full verbatim on demand — gist is the default served layer, verbatim is the expand-on-request layer. Distinct failure mode this subsystem names and guards: gist-based false memory — recalling the meaning but misremembering specifics. In pm terms, an agent that reasons only from a compacted gist can confidently assert a detail the verbatim never supported; the subsystem therefore requires that any gist digest carry a provenance pointer to the verbatim checkpoint it was derived from, and that gist/verbatim divergence be a detectable, queryable signal (algorithm below) rather than a silent drift. Composes with pm-4k6b: the write-time fold (6) maintains the gist digest per item at close time; the retrieval funnel (7) serves gist by default and prices verbatim expansion as a level upgrade in packing (9); reconsolidation (15) corrections attach at the gist layer so they survive verbatim compaction. Spectrum behavior: small/young workspaces have no old quiescent items so nothing is ever compacted — pure no-op, full verbatim always served; million-item multi-decade workspaces retain 100% of gist at a fraction of the bytes, with verbatim reconstructable per item on demand — the storage-side realisation of "project management = context management" where the CORPUS itself stays token-cheap to hold in context, not just each query's output. Acceptance shape when promoted: each closed item gains a derived, immutable gist digest with a verbatim-checkpoint provenance pointer; default retrieval serves gist; verbatim is expand-on-request and always fully reconstructable via history replay; a validate/health signal reports gist/verbatim divergence and un-provenanced gist; no verbatim bytes are ever destroyed.
